topic: Sharp partial identification of the average treatment effect on the treated (ATET) in staggered-adoption panel data under a total-variation (TV) bound on the pre-trend discrepancy -- replacing the sup-norm / Lipschitz smoothness relaxations of Rambachan & Roth (2023). Establish a closed-form sharp width in terms of the TV-discrepancy bound and the pre-treatment outcome dispersion, characterise the critical-TV-discrepancy frontier separating sign-identification from vacuity, and exhibit when the closed form strictly tightens the sup-norm bound.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the sharp closed-form ATET interval for staggered-adoption panels when the admissible post-treatment trend violation has bounded total variation relative to the observed pre-treatment discrepancy path.

source_refs.claim: Rambachan and Roth formulate Honest DiD as partial identification under researcher-chosen restrictions on post-treatment deviations from parallel trends, with relative-magnitude and smoothness examples but no TV-discrepancy closed form.
source_refs.author: Rambachan and Roth

source_refs.claim: Manski and Pepper use bounded-variation assumptions to conduct inference under restrictions of varying identifying power, supplying the partial-ID precedent for replacing exact DiD assumptions by variation bounds.
source_refs.author: Manski and Pepper

source_refs.excerpt: Sharp partial identification of staggered difference-in-differences under partially unobserved pre-trends
flagship rank-phase and discontinuity statements are Conjectures, not proved theorems

open_problems.why_unsolved: Rambachan--Roth leave the restriction set abstract enough to include alternatives, but their worked identifying geometries are relative-magnitude/sup-norm and second-difference smoothness classes. The in-repo pretrend legacy entry shows that CausalSmith has treated partially unobserved staggered-DID pretrends as a live partial-ID object, while the later Honest-DID phase proposal still leaves the support-function frontier conjectural. The missing obstacle is not another sensitivity parameter but the TV geometry itself: the extremal post-treatment deviation must be characterized under an l1-type variation budget tied to the signed pre-period discrepancy path and then lifted through staggered ATET aggregation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set theorem giving lower and upper ATET endpoints and closed-form width as functions of the TV-discrepancy bound, the signed pre-treatment discrepancy path, and the staggered-adoption aggregation weights, with a sharpness construction.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize when a TV-bound computed on an aggregate event-study path is the exact projection of the cohort-level staggered-adoption ATET sensitivity set rather than a conservative or anti-conservative relaxation.

source_refs.claim: Callaway and Sant'Anna define group-time treatment effects and aggregation schemes for staggered adoption under conditional parallel trends, but not TV sensitivity sharpness after aggregation.
source_refs.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: Sun and Abraham show that staggered event-study leads and lags can be contaminated by heterogeneous effects, so aggregate pretrend objects need not preserve cohort-level causal structure.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.claim: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess build imputation event-study estimators for staggered adoption, clarifying target aggregation but not the sharp support-function image of TV trend-deviation sets.
source_refs.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess

open_problems.why_unsolved: Modern staggered-DID papers identify and estimate cohort or aggregate ATT objects, but they do not decide whether a sensitivity restriction imposed after event-time aggregation is the same set as the projection of a cohort-event restriction. The CausalSmith Honest-DID phase proposal isolates exactly this quotient-support obstacle and leaves the sharp/conservative/anti-conservative trichotomy as an unproved rank-phase conjecture. For a TV bound, the obstacle is sharper because total variation can contract or expand under aggregation depending on cancellation of signed cohort discrepancies.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient linear-operator theorem comparing the cohort-level TV support function with the aggregate-path TV support function, including equality conditions and strict conservative/anti-conservative witness designs.

open_problems.open_problem: Give the critical-TV-discrepancy frontier at which a staggered-adoption ATET loses sign identification and, separately, the frontier at which the TV-restricted identified set becomes vacuous over the outcome range.

source_refs.claim: Rambachan and Roth recommend reporting breakdown parameter values at which causal conclusions are no longer supported, but their reported frontiers are tied to their RM/smoothness classes.
source_refs.author: Rambachan and Roth

source_refs.claim: Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe argue that sensitivity analysis should state what trend violations must be ruled out to sustain a causal conclusion.
source_refs.author: Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe

source_refs.excerpt: rank-loss discontinuity under cohort trimming (NOT YET PROVED)

open_problems.why_unsolved: Breakdown reporting is standard in the Honest-DID workflow, but the frontier is usually numerical and indexed by the published RM/smoothness parameter rather than an analytic TV radius. The in-repo phase proposal further warns that cohort trimming and active-face ties can make breakdown frontiers discontinuous. A TV frontier therefore has two unresolved pieces: the deterministic threshold where zero first enters the sharp ATET interval, and the larger-radius regime where the finite outcome-dispersion restriction leaves no sign information at all.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A closed-form formula for the minimal TV radius that makes zero feasible, a second formula or criterion for vacuity over the maintained outcome support, and examples showing continuity or jumps under cohort trimming.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify necessary and sufficient conditions under which the TV-discrepancy ATET interval is a strict subset of the Rambachan--Roth relative-magnitude sup-norm interval.

source_refs.claim: Their relative-magnitude class bounds post-treatment violations by a multiple of the maximum pre-treatment violation, leaving alternative discrepancy geometries to be supplied by the analyst.
source_refs.author: Rambachan and Roth

source_refs.claim: Kahn-Lang and Lang emphasize that parallel pretrends are informative but not dispositive, motivating sensitivity classes that use more than a pass/fail or max-deviation diagnostic.
source_refs.author: Kahn-Lang and Lang

source_refs.excerpt: sharp deterministic Chebyshev interval conditional on the observed signed pre-gap vector
strictly narrower and kinked differently from the rectangular outer envelope

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature contrasts sensitivity assumptions but does not compare the endpoint geometry of TV and sup-norm discrepancy sets for the same ATET target. CausalSmith has two adjacent lessons: vector-valued signed pre-gap information can beat scalar prefit summaries, and joint support functions can be strictly narrower than rectangular outer envelopes. The unresolved TV-specific obstacle is to translate those lessons into a precise inclusion theorem: a TV ball is not merely a smaller sup-norm ball, because its tightening depends on the ordering, signs, and dispersion of pre-period discrepancies.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An inclusion-and-strictness theorem giving conditions on the signed pretrend path and outcome dispersion under which the TV sharp interval is strictly contained in, equal to, or incomparable with the relative-magnitude sup-norm interval.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct TV-calibrated ATET identified sets that remain valid after pretrend diagnostics reject or have low power, without conditioning the causal interval on a passed pretrend test.

source_refs.claim: Roth shows that conventional pretrend tests can have low power and that conditioning on passing them can distort estimates and confidence intervals.
source_refs.author: Roth

source_refs.claim: Bilinski and Hatfield propose non-inferiority/equivalence testing for meaningful pretrend violations on the treatment-effect scale, but this diagnostic is not itself a sharp ATET identified set.
source_refs.author: Bilinski and Hatfield

source_refs.claim: The DiD review emphasizes sensitivity analysis as a complement to diagnostics, especially when exact parallel trends is not credible.
source_refs.author: Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe

open_problems.why_unsolved: Pretrend tests and equivalence diagnostics answer whether deviations are detectable or practically large, while the target here is a population identified set under a substantive TV restriction. The unresolved mathematical issue is how to use the observed pre-period discrepancy path as calibration data for the TV radius without turning the final ATET interval into a conditional-on-selection object that inherits pretest bias.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem or valid-inference construction that maps the pre-period discrepancy vector and a TV radius into a sharp ATET interval and sign frontier, with sampling uncertainty layered on without conditioning on test passage.

literature_map:
1. Manski and Pepper (2018, RESTAT) anchor bounded-variation partial identification for DiD-style policy ambiguity.
2. Freyaldenhoven, Hansen, and Shapiro (2019, AER) handle pre-event trends using proxy covariates for latent confounds.
3. Kahn-Lang and Lang (2020, JBES) stress that parallel pretrends are neither necessary nor sufficient for counterfactual parallel trends.
4. Roth (2022, AER Insights) shows low power and pretest bias in event-study pretrend testing.
5. Bilinski and Hatfield (2026, Statistics in Medicine) develop non-inferiority/equivalence tests for meaningful pretrend violations.
6. Rambachan and Roth (2023, RESTUD) give Honest DiD partial identification under RM/sup-norm and smoothness restrictions.
7. Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe (2023, Journal of Econometrics) synthesize DiD advances and motivate sensitivity analysis beyond diagnostics.
8. Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics) define group-time ATT estimands for staggered adoption under conditional parallel trends.
9. Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics) show TWFE event-study contamination and false apparent pretrends under heterogeneity.
10. de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) and Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics) decompose TWFE weighting pathologies.
11. Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, RESTUD) provide robust efficient imputation for staggered event studies under parallel trends.
12. Ding and Li (2019, Political Analysis) and Ye, Keele, Hasegawa, and Small (2024, JASA) develop bracketing-style partial identification alternatives.
13. Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, Econometrica) show parallel trends can be sensitive to outcome functional form, suggesting scale-aware sensitivity choices.
14. The unmet niche is a TV-bound, closed-form, sharp ATET width/frontier that connects pre-treatment dispersion to staggered-panel sensitivity.
prior_proposal_map: Closest direct overlap: panel_honest_did_phase_v1 is the main Honest-DiD/Rambachan-Roth adjacent source, with cohort-vs-aggregate support-function frontiers and breakdown-threshold phase language. pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 supplies a clean partial-ID staggered-DID support-interval template, though its anticipation-box gap is not the TV-pretrend target. Legacy q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends is directly pretrend/partial-DID adjacent but only a README pointer was present in the current tree. pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1 is the closest bounded pre-period gap / scalar-diagnostic nonsharpness source and is useful for strict tightening over sup-norm or scalar dispersion summaries. pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 contributes a generic lesson that joint support functions can be strictly tighter than rectangular envelopes. q_did_drdid_ipwdid_frontier_v1 and eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 are DID/frontier sources but mainly exact-ID or IF-equivalence, not the target TV partial-ID problem. Study-pipeline gaps were mostly LATE/Manski interval-regression substrate questions and did not directly overlap the TV-pretrend ATET anchor. No prior proposal found the exact qid pid_tv_pretrend_atet/v1 or a closed-form TV-discrepancy ATET width.
